brands.name: Jack in the Box
brands.notes: Major QSR burger chain actively recruiting multi-unit franchise partners across published target markets (franchise site: jackintheboxfranchising.com). Brand is mid-way through its 2025 'JACK on Track' turnaround, which includes closing 150-200 underperforming restaurants while selling new franchises in growth markets.

riskFlags.title: 'JACK on Track' block closure program (150-200 restaurants)
riskFlags.detail: In April 2025 Jack in the Box announced a plan to close 150-200 underperforming restaurants. The company closed 86 restaurants in FY2025, including 51 under the block closure program (38 in Q4, 13 in Q3).
riskFlags.source: Jack in the Box Inc. Form 8-K, Q4 FY2025 earnings release (SEC EDGAR)

riskFlags.title: Franchisee association no-confidence vote and breach-of-contract lawsuit (2019-2021, settled)
riskFlags.detail: The National Jack in the Box Franchisee Association passed a no-confidence vote in then-CEO Lenny Comma (Oct 2019) and sued the franchisor for breach of contract over marketing-fund audit rights and remodel cost-shifting. The suit was settled in 2021 with concessions to franchisees under new CEO Darin Harris.

riskFlags.title: Del Taco divested December 2025
riskFlags.detail: As part of the JACK on Track plan, Jack in the Box completed the sale of sister brand Del Taco to Yadav Enterprises for approximately $119 million on December 22, 2025, refocusing the company on the core Jack in the Box brand.
riskFlags.source: Jack in the Box Inc. Form 8-K (SEC EDGAR)

avgUnitRevenue.note: Average yearly gross sales per restaurant per FDD Item 19 analysis; franchisor advertises ~$1.9M AUV.

totalUnits.note: Systemwide; roughly 9 of 10 restaurants are franchised. Unit count trending about -1% amid JACK on Track closures.

closureCount.source: Jack in the Box Inc. Form 8-K, Q4 FY2025 earnings release (SEC EDGAR)
closureCount.note: Restaurants closed in FY2025, including 51 under the JACK on Track block closure program.

yearFranchising.note: Entrepreneur lists franchising since 1982; some FDD-summary sources cite earlier franchising activity.

trainingWeeks.note: Initial training approximately 6-8 weeks, conducted in San Diego, CA.

multiUnitAvailable.source: Jack in the Box Franchising (franchisor site)
multiUnitAvailable.note: 3+ store commitments earn 2% royalty reduction for 5 years or $150,000 interest-free loan; average franchisee owns 15+ units.

isGrowing.note: US unit count contracting (~-1%) during the 2025-2026 JACK on Track closure program, even as new franchise markets are sold.

brands.name: Bojangles
brands.notes: Southeastern chicken-and-biscuits chain actively selling franchises and expanding into new markets (bojangles.com/franchising redirects to bojanglesfranchising.com). The franchisor publishes an interactive territories map (Available/Future/Reserved markets) but no public state-by-state list, so state availability is omitted here.

riskFlags.title: Franchisee association lawsuit over marketing fund and undisclosed 'accrual account'
riskFlags.detail: The Bojangles of America Franchisee Association (30 franchisees operating 380+ restaurants) sued Bojangles Opco in NC Superior Court for breach of contract and unfair/deceptive trade practices, alleging the company blocked a promised audit of the marketing fund and concealed an 'accrual account' funded by franchisee contributions that was not disclosed in the FDD.

riskFlags.title: Bojangles sued its largest franchise group
riskFlags.detail: Bojangles filed suit against its largest franchise group and an accounting firm in 2024, indicating active franchisor-franchisee litigation in both directions.

riskFlags.title: Maryland franchisee collapse closed 5 locations
riskFlags.detail: A Bojangles franchisee closed all 5 of its Maryland locations in late 2023; former employees alleged gross financial mismanagement including wage theft and fraudulent W-2s. Reflects single-operator failure rather than systemwide issues.

liquidCapitalMin.note: $1,000,000 required liquidity and $2,500,000 required net worth per location.

totalInvestmentMin.note: Express format low end; traditional restaurants run $2,851,880-$3,951,200. 2025 FDD-derived range: $778,670-$3,829,400.

franchiseFee.source: Bojangles 2025 FDD (via FDD.ninja and Franchise Direct FDD summary)
franchiseFee.note: Standard fee for a traditional restaurant; Express restaurant fee is $20,000.

adFundPercent.note: 1% national Marketing Development Fund; franchisees must additionally spend 3% of gross sales on local marketing (less co-op amounts). Directories often quote the combined 4%.

avgUnitRevenue.source: Bojangles 2024 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter FDD Talk review)
avgUnitRevenue.note: Average gross sales of 448 franchised full-size restaurants with bone-in chicken menu, fiscal 2023 (53 weeks ended 12/31/2023). Express franchised average: $2,117,580.

totalUnits.note: Systemwide, roughly +7.4% trend; 2024 FDD showed 528 franchised + 285 company-owned at end of 2023.

trainingWeeks.note: Initial program 13-26 weeks for managing owners/operators; managers get 4-8 weeks at Bojangles University in Charlotte, NC.

veteranDiscount.note: Qualified veteran, women, and minority franchisees get 50% off the franchise fee and reduced royalty for the first three years.

multiUnitAvailable.note: Development agreements for 3+ and 5+ unit operators with equipment reimbursement incentives.

isGrowing.source: Franchise Chatter FDD Talk review (2024 FDD outlet tables); Entrepreneur
isGrowing.note: Franchised units grew 496 -> 507 -> 528 across 2021-2023; Entrepreneur shows +7.4% trend.

brands.name: Qdoba Mexican Eats
brands.notes: Fast-casual Mexican chain actively selling franchises across US markets (franchise site: qdobafranchise.com) with 450+ future restaurants under signed development commitments and a minimum two-restaurant commitment for new franchisees.

riskFlags.title: Franchisor-franchisee litigation over failed opening (Fiesta Ventures)
riskFlags.detail: A franchisee whose Beavercreek, OH restaurant failed to open on time was defaulted by Qdoba in Feb 2024 and sued alleging breach of contract and unfair business practices; in Aug 2025 a California federal court allowed Qdoba's counter-claims for lost future royalties to proceed.
riskFlags.source: Goldstein Law Firm franchise litigation commentary

riskFlags.title: Historical contraction under prior ownership
riskFlags.detail: Under former parent Jack in the Box, Qdoba moved to close 67 stores companywide in 2017 before being sold. Current ownership reports 16 consecutive quarters of positive same-store sales through 2024 and net franchised-unit growth (+42 in FY2024 per the 2025 FDD).

nationwide.source: QDOBA press release: new franchise development agreements across the United States; qdobafranchise.com available territories
nationwide.note: Actively selling franchises in markets across the US (450+ restaurant commitments signed); specific territories subject to availability via the franchisor's Available Territories page. Minimum two-restaurant commitment.

liquidCapitalMin.source: QDOBA Franchise (franchisor site, What It Takes)

totalInvestmentMin.note: Traditional restaurant, excluding real property and liquor license; non-traditional from $236,500. Franchisor site currently shows $249,100-$1,299,000 for traditional.

royaltyPercent.source: Qdoba 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review); franchisor site
royaltyPercent.note: 5% of gross sales for traditional restaurants (franchisor site lists 6% for the non-traditional model).

adFundPercent.note: Marketing fee 2.75% of gross sales plus 1.25% required local advertising spend (combined ~4%).

avgUnitRevenue.source: Qdoba 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter review)
avgUnitRevenue.note: Average net restaurant sales for 318 traditional franchised restaurants reporting a trailing 12-month period; individual results vary.

totalUnits.source: Qdoba 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter review)

veteranDiscount.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory; franchisor VetFran membership
veteranDiscount.note: $10,000 off the initial franchise fee for honorably discharged veterans.

multiUnitAvailable.note: Franchisees must commit to opening a minimum of two restaurants.

isGrowing.source: Qdoba 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter); QDOBA press release
isGrowing.note: +42 net franchised outlets in FY2024 and 450+ signed future-restaurant commitments; company-owned units declined by 12 as units refranchised.

brands.name: Tim Hortons
brands.notes: Canada's largest QSR chain (parent: Restaurant Brands International) actively selling US franchises via franchising.timhortons.com, with core availability in the Midwest/Northeast and announced expansion into Southern markets. States listed below are those with explicit named development evidence; the franchisor maintains a broader available-markets page.

riskFlags.title: Major franchisee association lawsuits (Great White North Franchisee Association)
riskFlags.detail: US and Canadian franchisee associations sued Tim Hortons/RBI in 2018, alleging improper use of the advertising fund (seeking $500M in the Canadian action), supply 'price gouging,' intimidation of association members, and ~$450,000 forced remodel costs. Over 70% of Canadian franchisees had joined the GWNFA at its peak.

riskFlags.title: Flat US franchised unit count despite expansion push
riskFlags.detail: Per the 2025 FDD, US franchised locations saw a net change of -2 in 2024 (after +6 in 2023 and -1 in 2022), ending 2024 at 640 franchised units, even as parent RBI targets large-scale US growth.

riskFlags.title: High combined ongoing fees and mandatory refurbishment
riskFlags.detail: Royalty of 4.5%-6% plus a 4% advertising contribution puts total ongoing fees as high as 10% of sales, above typical QSR norms, and franchisees face a mandatory refurbishment cycle every 5 years.

statesOpen.note: Largest US market; Manhattan (NYC) named among new markets opening before 2026.
statesOpen.source: QSR Magazine - Tim Hortons U.S. Ready to Unlock the Future After Breakthrough Year

statesOpen.note: Bradenton, FL named as a new development market.
statesOpen.source: QSR Magazine - Tim Hortons U.S. Ready to Unlock the Future After Breakthrough Year

statesOpen.note: College Station, TX named as a new market; Austin opened Dec 2024.
statesOpen.source: QSR Magazine - Tim Hortons U.S. Ready to Unlock the Future After Breakthrough Year

statesOpen.note: Nashville cited among performing 2025 new-market locations.
statesOpen.source: QSR Magazine - Tim Hortons U.S. Ready to Unlock the Future After Breakthrough Year

statesOpen.note: Norfolk cited among performing 2025 new-market locations.
statesOpen.source: QSR Magazine - Tim Hortons U.S. Ready to Unlock the Future After Breakthrough Year

statesOpen.note: Core Midwest growth market (204 locations, second-largest US state).
statesOpen.source: Tim Hortons US Franchising - Available Markets

statesOpen.note: Core Midwest growth market per franchisor's US franchising site.
statesOpen.source: Tim Hortons US Franchising - Available Markets

liquidCapitalMin.note: Cash requirement listed as $500,000-$4,000,000; net worth $1,500,000-$10,000,000.

totalInvestmentMin.source: Tim Hortons 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)
totalInvestmentMin.note: Standard shop formats; non-standard shops (kiosks etc.) can start far lower (directory sources cite from ~$246,500). Freestanding drive-thru builds run $1,017,600-$1,370,500.

totalInvestmentMax.source: Tim Hortons 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)

franchiseFee.source: Tim Hortons 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter and Franchise Direct summaries)
franchiseFee.note: Standard shop; $25,000 for a non-standard shop. 20-year franchise agreement term.

royaltyPercent.source: Tim Hortons 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)
royaltyPercent.note: Standard royalty ranges 4.5%-6% of gross sales depending on the underlying real estate/lease arrangement; 4.5% is the low end, not a discounted tier.

adFundPercent.source: Tim Hortons 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)

avgUnitRevenue.source: Tim Hortons 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter review)
avgUnitRevenue.note: Annualized from the disclosed $105,737 average monthly sales for 579 US standard shops open as of 12/31/2024; derived figure, not stated as an annual AUV in the FDD.

totalUnits.source: Tim Hortons 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter review)
totalUnits.note: US only: 640 franchised + 23 company-owned at end of 2024. Systemwide (incl. Canada/international) is ~5,000+ units.

brands.name: Auntie Anne's
brands.notes: World's largest soft-pretzel franchise (GoTo Foods, formerly Focus Brands), actively recruiting US franchisees via development.gotofoods.com/auntie-annes. The franchisor does not publish a state-by-state availability list (one broker portal notes inquiries are demand-gated), so state availability is omitted pending FDD confirmation.

riskFlags.title: Venue/landlord dependence (malls, outlets, airports)
riskFlags.detail: Unit economics hinge on foot traffic the franchisee does not control, in space leased from landlords with leverage; 2024 average net sales ranged from $763K (enclosed mall) to $1.8M (airport), showing heavy location-driven variability.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - Auntie Anne's Franchise Insights

riskFlags.title: Royalty can be raised to 8% at franchisor's sole discretion; mandatory remodel cycle
riskFlags.detail: The 7% royalty may be increased to 8% of Net Sales at Auntie Anne's sole discretion, and franchisees must refresh shops every 5 years and fully remodel every 10 years - ongoing costs not captured in headline investment figures.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Chatter 2025 FDD review; Vetted Biz

riskFlags.title: 41 closures in 2024 (about 3.4% of units)
riskFlags.detail: 41 franchised closures against ~1,193 total units in 2024 - a closure rate below the food-category average - alongside 52 transfers, suggesting units mostly sell as going concerns; system still grew +26 net franchised units.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - Auntie Anne's Franchise Insights

liquidCapitalMin.note: Minimum liquid capital $120,000; minimum net worth $300,000.

totalInvestmentMin.source: Auntie Anne's 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review; Entrepreneur)
totalInvestmentMin.note: Range covers kiosk/mall through larger formats; franchisor site cites $158,000-$836,000 for enclosed-mall builds in 2026.

totalInvestmentMax.source: Auntie Anne's 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review; Entrepreneur)

franchiseFee.source: Auntie Anne's 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review; Entrepreneur)
franchiseFee.note: Standard full-shop fee; reduced fees apply to some venue/secondary formats.

royaltyPercent.source: Auntie Anne's 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)
royaltyPercent.note: 7% of Net Sales; franchisor may raise to 8% at its sole discretion.

adFundPercent.source: Auntie Anne's 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)
adFundPercent.note: 2% of Net Sales for most locations (3% streetside; co-branded varies), plus a 1% local marketing spend obligation each quarter.

avgUnitRevenue.source: Auntie Anne's 2025 FDD Item 19, FY2024 (via Franchise Chatter review)
avgUnitRevenue.note: Average net sales for 498 enclosed-mall franchised shops (largest cohort) in FY2024. Other venues: outlet center $922,671; airport $1,816,883; Cinnabon co-branded $1,200,635.

totalUnits.source: Auntie Anne's 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter review)
totalUnits.note: US: 1,182 franchised + 12 company-owned at end of 2024; 1,200+ locations globally across 20+ countries (2,087 systemwide per Entrepreneur, incl. international).

yearFounded.note: Founded by Anne Beiler at a Pennsylvania farmers market.

trainingWeeks.note: Approximately two weeks of classes plus on-the-job experience in a high-volume store (Entrepreneur lists 60 OTJ + 25 classroom hours).

closureCount.source: Vetted Biz - Auntie Anne's Franchise Insights
closureCount.note: Franchised closures in 2024 (~3.4% of units, below category average).

brands.name: Cold Stone Creamery
brands.notes: Kahala Brands ice-cream franchise actively selling US franchises nationwide via coldstonecreameryfranchise.com, with a stated initiative to add hundreds of locations. Strong recent net unit growth, but the brand carries a well-documented history of franchisee failures and SBA-loan defaults from the 2004-2013 era that buyers should weigh.

riskFlags.title: Historically among the worst SBA loan default rates in franchising
riskFlags.detail: WSJ-reported SBA data showed Cold Stone franchisee loan failure rates of roughly 31%-42% across 2004-2013, more than double typical franchise default rates and among the 10 worst brands in the SBA portfolio. Data is historical (pre-2014, spanning prior ownership) but reflects structural unit-economics risk.
riskFlags.source: Law Offices of Spotora & Associates summary of WSJ SBA-default analysis

riskFlags.title: Longstanding franchisee complaints about unit economics
riskFlags.detail: Failed and current franchisees have alleged thin margins driven by mandatory two-for-one couponing/promotions, required purchases from designated suppliers, franchise 'churning,' and inadequate support. Allegations are documented in franchisee-complaint forums and a CHRIE academic case study.
riskFlags.source: Unhappy Franchisee - Cold Stone Creamery Franchise Complaints

riskFlags.title: Recent net unit growth contrasts with historical contraction
riskFlags.detail: Per the 2025 FDD, US franchised locations grew from 889 (start of 2022) to 992 (end of Nov 2024) - net +19, +44, and +40 across the three years - indicating the system is currently expanding.

nationwide.source: Cold Stone Creamery franchise site (Kahala Brands)
nationwide.note: Franchisor states it is 'rolling out a development initiative with the aim of growing the brand by hundreds of locations' and accepts franchise inquiries from all 50 states plus DC.

liquidCapitalMin.note: Franchisor site lists $125,000 liquid / $250,000 net worth; Entrepreneur lists $120,000 cash requirement.

totalInvestmentMin.source: Cold Stone Creamery 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review; Entrepreneur)
totalInvestmentMin.note: Traditional store range; franchisor site in 2026 shows traditional $390,675-$680,775 and non-traditional from $255,700.

totalInvestmentMax.source: Cold Stone Creamery 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review; Entrepreneur)

franchiseFee.source: Cold Stone Creamery franchise site; 2025 FDD summaries
franchiseFee.note: Standard fee for a traditional store; FDD discloses a $12,000-$27,000 range by store type/term ($8,000-$20,000 non-traditional).

royaltyPercent.source: Cold Stone Creamery 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)

adFundPercent.source: Cold Stone Creamery 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)

avgUnitRevenue.source: Cold Stone Creamery 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter review)
avgUnitRevenue.note: Average gross sales across 939 US franchised stores open the full fiscal year 12/1/2023-11/30/2024; median $587,242.

totalUnits.source: Cold Stone Creamery 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter review)
totalUnits.note: US franchised outlets as of 11/30/2024; ~1,481 systemwide globally per Entrepreneur (~30 countries).

yearFounded.note: Founded by Donald and Susan Sutherland in Tempe, AZ.
